scores.justification: The explanation discusses quantifier reasoning, but not the specific conservativity phenomenon. It is only partially sound: from 'every Italian tenor wants to become great' alone you cannot infer existence; the existential support comes from the second premise that some Italian tenors exist (and are great). It still supports the entailment label.

Its reasoning is logically sound: 'Ένας Ιταλός...' entails that there existed an Italian with that property. It clearly supports the Entailment label.

It still supports the entailment label.

It is also logically unsound in standard NLI semantics because 'All A are B' does not entail existence of any A, so the move to 'Some A are B' is invalid. Still, it does support the predicted entailment label.
